title: Parapolydora fastigiata

taxonomy: Parapolydora fastigiata is a species of polychaete worm, specifically belonging to the family Spionidae. This classification is based on its physical characteristics and molecular analysis. The Spionidae family is a group of marine worms that are commonly found in a variety of habitats, including sandy and muddy substrates. At the higher taxonomic level, Parapolydora fastigiata is classified as a member of the phylum Annelida, which includes segmented worms. This classification system is used to organize and understand the relationships between different species within the animal kingdom.
